CAR-1,"Scaffold index.html: boilerplate, full-window canvas, tunables block, game loop","done","P1","standard","small","large","G1","","index.html","No file exists yet. Need the foundation: an HTML document with a full-window canvas, inline CSS, a clearly-labelled tunables/constants block at the top of the script, and a requestAnimationFrame loop with a delta-time clamped by a named MAX_DT constant.","index.html opens in a browser to a full-window canvas that clears each frame at ~60fps via a requestAnimationFrame loop using a delta-time clamped to MAX_DT. All gameplay tunables live in one UPPER_SNAKE_CASE constants block at the very top of the <script>, using the canonical names listed in notes.","grep -c 'requestAnimationFrame' index.html >=1
column2: grep -c '<canvas' index.html == 1
column3: canvas resizes to window via a resize handler (grep -c ""addEventListener('resize'"" index.html >=1)
column4: [C1] all tunables present — for C in ACCEL REVERSE_ACCEL TOP_SPEED TOP_REVERSE_SPEED FRICTION STEER_RATE GRIP HANDBRAKE_GRIP OBSTACLE_COUNT ARENA_SIZE RESTITUTION GRID_SIZE SKID_THRESHOLD SKID_MAX MAX_DT
column7: [C2] dt clamped by MAX_DT (grep -qE 'dt *= *Math\.min' index.html && grep -qE 'MAX_DT' index.html)
column8: opening the file shows a non-erroring blank canvas (manual: no console errors).","[H2] Canonical tunable names — declare EXACTLY these (no slash-alternatives): ACCEL REVERSE_ACCEL TOP_SPEED TOP_REVERSE_SPEED FRICTION STEER_RATE GRIP HANDBRAKE_GRIP OBSTACLE_COUNT ARENA_SIZE RESTITUTION GRID_SIZE SKID_THRESHOLD SKID_MAX MAX_DT. RESTITUTION (not WALL_BOUNCE) and FRICTION (not DRAG) are the locked names downstream rows reference. [C2] MAX_DT is the dt cap const (e.g. 0.033)
column9: loop computes dt = Math.min(MAX_DT, (now-last)/1000) for stability on slow frames. [M1] Document GRIP valid range: GRIP*MAX_DT < 1 (i.e. GRIP < ~30 at MAX_DT=0.033) so the lateral scale stays non-negative. SKID_THRESHOLD/SKID_MAX live here so the constants block is the single source of truth — downstream rows must not add tunables mid-file. Foundation for all other rows.

column1: CAR-2,"World-space camera that follows the car + tiled grid floor","done","P1","standard","small","medium","G1","CAR-1","index.html","The loop draws in screen space with no world. Need a camera transform centered on the car and a repeating grid drawn in world coordinates so motion is visible.","A camera centered on the car translates the canvas so the car stays at screen center
column2: a grid of GRID_SIZE cells is drawn in world space and visibly scrolls past as the car moves, giving a clear sense of speed and direction.","grep -E 'translate\(' index.html >=1 (camera transform applied)
column4: [L2] grid drawn relative to world origin via modulo on GRID_SIZE (grep -E '% *GRID_SIZE' index.html >=1)
column5: with the car moving, grid lines visibly scroll (manual: drive and confirm lines move opposite to travel)
column6: car remains centered on screen at all times (manual observation).","Camera follows car: cameraX = car.x - canvas.width/2, cameraY = car.y - canvas.height/2 (ctx.translate(-cameraX,-cameraY)). Grid drawn only across the visible viewport using modulo on GRID_SIZE to avoid drawing the whole arena. [F-CAR2-01] Draw a MANDATORY placeholder car here (colored rect rotated to heading) so the scene is visible
column7: CAR-3 REPLACES this placeholder draw block with the physics-based car draw — exactly one car draw call after CAR-3.

column1: CAR-3,"Car arcade physics: momentum, friction, lateral grip/drift, speed-scaled steering","done","P0","critical","medium","large","G2","CAR-2","index.html","The car has no physics. Need a velocity-vector arcade model: throttle/brake/reverse along heading, friction so it coasts, separate lateral grip so it can drift, steering that only works while moving and scales with speed, and a handbrake that cuts grip.","The car accumulates velocity from throttle along its heading, coasts and slows via FRICTION when input released, and slides/drifts because lateral velocity is only partially killed each frame by GRIP. Steering rate is ~0 at rest and scales with speed. Handbrake (car.handbrake flag) drops grip toward HANDBRAKE_GRIP so the rear slides. Speed is capped at TOP_SPEED (and TOP_REVERSE_SPEED in reverse).","Physics decomposes velocity into forward + lateral components relative to heading (grep -E 'cos\(|sin\(' and a lateral/forward split in the update)
column2: [M1] GRIP applied to lateral velocity with a non-negative clamp (grep -E 'Math\.max\(0' near the GRIP lateral scale)
column3: steering scaled by speed and disabled at rest (grep -E 'speed|vel' guarding the steer term
column4: manual: car will not rotate when stationary)
column7: exactly one car draw call (CAR-2 placeholder block replaced, not duplicated)
column8: manual: release throttle -> car coasts to a stop
column9: hard turn at speed -> visible drift
column11: manual reverse-steer check: hold S past a stop into reverse, then steer — confirm A/D invert per the locked decision in notes.","LOAD-BEARING correctness row. [H1] PINNED CONTRACTS for downstream rows: input held-state map = `keys` (object keyed by event.code, produced by CAR-4)
column12: handbrake flag = `car.handbrake` (bool, set true while keys['Space'] held)
column13: lateral slip exposed as `car.lateralSlip` (for CAR-6). CAR-4 and CAR-7 must use these exact names. Sub-checklist (verify each) [F-CAR3-02]: (1) throttle adds ACCEL*dt along heading (cos,sin)
column14: (2) FRICTION decays forward speed when coasting
column15: (3) decompose velocity into forward + lateral axes
column16: (4) [M1] lateral *= Math.max(0, 1 - GRIP*dt) so some slide remains = drift AND factor never goes negative
column17: (5) steer: car.heading += STEER_RATE * steerInput * clamp(speed/TOP_SPEED) * sign(forwardVelocity) * dt, ~0 at rest
column20: (8) clamp speed to TOP_SPEED (TOP_REVERSE_SPEED in reverse). [F-CAR2-01] Replace the CAR-2 placeholder car draw with the physics car draw — exactly one draw call. qa_tier-override: bumped standard->critical — physics is the highest-bug-risk surface

column1: User decision: reverse-steer = INVERTS in reverse (sign(forwardVelocity) kept) — turning while backing up feels natural, standard arcade behavior. Locked default
column2: the alternative (Math.max(0,sign) for same-direction steer) was rejected. Applies to this row only.

column1: User decision: drift model = partial-lateral-grip (velocity-vector) arcade physics, locked in Step 1 brainstorm. No alternative to choose at execution time.

column1: CAR-4,"Keyboard input: WASD/arrows throttle-brake-steer, Space handbrake, R reset","done","P1","standard","small","large","G2","CAR-3","index.html","Physics has no driver. Need keydown/keyup wiring populating the `keys` held-state map (event.code) for WASD+arrows throttle/brake-reverse/steer, Space -> car.handbrake, and R -> reset the car to arena center with zero velocity.","Holding W/Up accelerates, S/Down brakes then reverses, A/D and Left/Right steer (only effective while moving per CAR-3), Space sets car.handbrake true while held, and R respawns the car at arena center with zero velocity and zero heading-rate. Keys populate the `keys` map (keyed by event.code) read by the physics update
column2: default browser scrolling for arrows/space is prevented.","grep -E ""addEventListener\('keydown'"" index.html >=1 and keyup >=1
column3: [H1] the held-state map is named `keys` and keyed by event.code (grep -E 'keys\[' index.html >=1)
column4: Space and arrow defaults prevented (grep -E 'preventDefault' index.html >=1)
column6: R resets car (grep -E ""'r'|'R'|KeyR"" near a reset that zeroes velocity)
column7: manual: W accelerates, S reverses, A/D steer while moving, Space handbrake, R respawns at center.","[H1] Populate the canonical `keys` map keyed by event.code on keydown(true)/keyup(false)
column8: the physics update (CAR-3) reads it each frame — do not apply forces in the event handler. Set car.handbrake = !!keys['Space'] (or in the physics read). preventDefault on Arrow*/Space to stop page scroll. R reset zeroes car.vx, car.vy, heading rate and recenters to ARENA_SIZE/2. [F-CAR4-01] Use event.code==='KeyR' (or event.key 'r'/'R') — NOT deprecated keyCode 82.

column1: CAR-5,"Bounded arena walls + ~10 scattered obstacles with bounce collision","done","P0","critical","medium","large","G3","CAR-4","index.html","The world is empty and unbounded. Need a finite walled arena and ~OBSTACLE_COUNT (~10) static obstacles the car collides with and bounces off, plus walls at the arena edges that bounce the same way.","OBSTACLE_COUNT obstacles are scattered across the arena (drawn, not overlapping spawn) and the car cannot pass through them — on contact it is pushed out and its velocity reflects (scaled by RESTITUTION) so it bounces away. The four arena walls bound the play area and bounce the car identically.","grep -E 'OBSTACLE_COUNT' index.html >=1
column2: obstacles generated into an array (grep -E 'obstacles\b' index.html >=1)
column3: [H2] collision resolves position (push-out) AND reflects velocity using RESTITUTION (grep -E 'car\.vx\s*\*=\s*-RESTITUTION|car\.vy\s*\*=\s*-RESTITUTION' index.html >=1)
column4: walls clamp+bounce car at arena bounds (grep -E 'ARENA_SIZE' in a bounds check)
column5: manual: drive into an obstacle -> car bounces off, does not tunnel through
column6: drive into a wall -> bounces, cannot leave arena.","Circle-vs-rect or AABB/circle collision is fine for arcade feel. On overlap: push the car out along the collision normal, then reflect the velocity component along that normal scaled by RESTITUTION. Walls = clamp car position to [r, ARENA_SIZE-r] and invert the corresponding velocity axis: car.vx *= -RESTITUTION / car.vy *= -RESTITUTION. [H2] Use the locked name RESTITUTION (declared in CAR-1) — do NOT introduce WALL_BOUNCE. Obstacle spawn should avoid the center spawn point. qa_tier-override: bumped standard->critical — collision/tunneling is the second-highest bug-risk surface

column1: CAR-6,"Skid marks painted to the ground on drift / handbrake","done","P2","standard","small","medium","G3","CAR-5","index.html","Drift is invisible. When the car's lateral slip is high (sharp turn at speed) or the handbrake is engaged, it should leave tire trails on the ground.","When lateral slip (car.lateralSlip) exceeds SKID_THRESHOLD (or car.handbrake is held while moving), skid mark segments are recorded at the car's rear-wheel positions in world space and drawn between the grid and the car, persisting (capped to SKID_MAX so memory is bounded).","grep -E 'SKID_THRESHOLD|skid' index.html >=1
column2: skid marks stored in a world-space array with a bounded cap (grep -E 'skid' and a cap via SKID_MAX / shift / slice)
column3: skid trigger keyed on car.lateralSlip or car.handbrake (grep -E 'car\.lateralSlip|car\.handbrake' near skid push)
column4: [M2] explicit final draw order grid -> skids -> obstacles -> car -> HUD
column5: car draw call appears AFTER the skid draw call in source order (manual/line-order check)
column6: manual: hard drift or handbrake-turn leaves visible trails that stay on the ground as the camera moves.","Read the car.lateralSlip value exposed in CAR-3 rather than recomputing. Record two points (rear corners) per frame while skidding
column7: cap array length at SKID_MAX and drop oldest. [M2] Canonical final draw order is: grid -> skids -> obstacles -> car -> HUD(CAR-7). Inserting skids requires the skid draw to precede the car draw established in CAR-2/CAR-3 — move/verify the car draw comes last among world objects. World-space so trails stay put under camera-follow. [F-CAR6-01] depends_on CAR-5 is for SERIAL ORDERING ONLY (single shared file) — the technical dependency is car.lateralSlip (CAR-3) + world-space render (CAR-2)
column8: obstacle code from CAR-5 is NOT needed for skids. SKID_THRESHOLD/SKID_MAX already exist in the CAR-1 constants block.

column1: CAR-7,"HUD: speed readout + input/handbrake state + controls legend","in_progress","P2","standard","small","medium","G3","CAR-6","index.html","No HUD. Need current speed shown in a corner, plus the richer state (throttle/brake/handbrake indicators) and a controls legend, drawn in screen space (not affected by the camera).","A fixed-position HUD in a screen corner shows numeric speed, live throttle/brake/handbrake state (reading car.handbrake and the keys map), and a small controls legend (WASD/Arrows, Space, R). It is drawn after the camera transform is reset so it stays put while the world scrolls.","HUD drawn in screen space — camera transform reset before HUD (grep -E 'setTransform\(1' index.html >=1)
column2: [L3] speed value rendered (grep -E 'fillText\([^)]*[Ss]peed' index.html >=1)
column3: [H1] handbrake/throttle/brake state shown, handbrake read from car.handbrake (grep -E 'car\.handbrake' index.html >=1 and grep -E 'throttle|brake' in HUD draw)
column4: controls legend present (grep -E 'WASD|Arrows|Space|Reset' in HUD text)
column5: manual: speed reads ~0 at rest and rises with throttle
column7: HUD stays fixed in the corner while driving.","Reset the camera transform with ctx.setTransform(1,0,0,1,0,0) before drawing HUD so it is screen-fixed [F-CAR7-01]. Speed = magnitude of velocity vector (Math.sqrt(vx*vx+vy*vy)), displayed rounded. [H1] Read car.handbrake and the `keys` map for the state indicators — same canonical names as CAR-3/CAR-4. [F-CAR7-02] 'Richer HUD' scope is DEFINED as: numeric speed + live throttle/brake/handbrake indicators + controls legend. No lap timer, score, or other metric required. Final row
column8: with it the demo meets all success criteria.
